identity.name: E1 - The Anti-AI Designer
identity.description: A focused, minimal design language that prioritizes data legibility using strict grids, monochromatic foundations, and deliberate asymmetry.

heading.usage: Use for all section titles, card headers, and major labels.

body.usage: Use for general text, labels, and descriptions.

mono.usage: MANDATORY for all currency values, percentages, and tabular data.

layout.grid_system: Use a 12-column grid. KPI cards span 3 columns on desktop, 6 on tablet, 12 on mobile.

kpi_card.style: bg-white border border-border shadow-sm rounded-xl p-6 hover:shadow-md transition-all duration-200
kpi_card.structure: Label (top, uppercase) -> Value (middle, mono, huge) -> Trend/Icon (bottom, subtle)
kpi_card.highlight: Use a subtle left border strip (4px) in brand color for active/primary KPIs.

chart_card.style: bg-white border border-border shadow-sm rounded-xl p-6
chart_card.header: Flex row, Title left, Action/Filter right. Minimal divider.

button.primary: bg-slate-900 text-white hover:bg-slate-800 shadow-sm rounded-lg px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors
button.ghost: bg-transparent text-slate-600 hover:bg-slate-100 hover:text-slate-900 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors
button.refresh: Use a ghost button with a rotation animation on click.

table.header: bg-slate-50 text-slate-500 uppercase font-bold text-xs tracking-wider border-b border-border py-3 px-4

universal_guidelines: DO NOT use gradients for backgrounds. Keep it flat and clean.
DO NOT center text in cards. Left align everything for better scanability.
Use 'JetBrains Mono' for ALL numbers, currency, and percentages.
KPI Cards must be simple: Label (small) + Number (Big) + Context (tiny).
Charts must use the monochrome+blue palette defined in colors.charts.
The Refresh button must be visible in the header.
Ensure the Date Filter is prominent and easy to use (use Shadcn Select or Popover).
All components must use 'rounded-xl' except where specified otherwise.
instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Install dependencies: `npm install lucide-react recharts` or `yarn add lucide-react recharts`.
3. Create a `DashboardLayout` component that handles the max-width and padding.
5. Use the defined color palette in `tailwind.config.js` or directly in classes.
6. Mock the Excel data processing if the backend isn't ready, but ensure the frontend expects the exact fields listed in the prompt.
7. Implement the 'Refresh' button to trigger a re-fetch of data.
8. Use Recharts for the visualizations: AreaChart for Revenue Trend, BarChart for Unit Breakdown.
9. Ensure mobile responsiveness: Grid 1 col on mobile, 4 cols on desktop.
